description.task: Implement an ArrayFundamentals class that demonstrates array declaration, initialization, and indexing. Create methods to initialize an array with squares, retrieve elements by index safely (with bounds check), and compute the sum of all elements.
instructions: initializeSquares(n): return new int[n] where arr[i] = (i+1)*(i+1) for i in 0..n-1
safeGet(arr, index): if index >= 0 && index < arr.length return arr[index], else return -1
sum(arr): iterate all elements and accumulate total
All three methods must be implemented
description.hint: initializeSquares: int[] arr = new int[n]; for(int i=0;i<n;i++) arr[i]=(i+1)*(i+1); return arr;

description.task: Create a MemoryLayout class that simulates the contiguous memory layout of a Java int array. Assume each int occupies 4 bytes starting at a base address of 1000. Given an index, compute its address. Print the memory map of an entire array.
instructions: printMemoryMap() calls addressOf() for each index — do not modify it
Only addressOf() needs to be implemented
Output format must match the header/separator/row pattern exactly
description.hint: return BASE_ADDRESS + index * INT_SIZE; That's all for addressOf().

description.task: Implement a StaticVsDynamic class that compares a fixed-size int[] (static) with an ArrayList<Integer> (dynamic). Demonstrate that the static array throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException on overflow while the ArrayList grows. Count capacity doublings when filling an ArrayList.
instructions: staticOverflows() is already implemented — do not change it
countCapacityDoublings(targetSize): start capacity at 10, double it while capacity < targetSize, count doublings
Return the number of times capacity was doubled
description.hint: int capacity=10, count=0; while(capacity < targetSize){ capacity*=2; count++; } return count;
description.expectedOutput:
Static overflows? true
Doublings for 100 elements: 4
Doublings for 1000 elements: 7
